description: Builder: simple, clear document outlining the costs of having kids. Include: financial costs...
detail: The document is ready — a clean, honest, five-section breakdown of the real costs of having kids, covering financial figures (birth through college), time and career trade-offs, lifestyle changes, and the things that don't show up in any spreadsheet, plus six conversation-starter questions designed to make it immediately useful for the two of them.

builder_result.artifact_title: The Real Cost of Having Kids
decisions_made: Organized into five sections: Financial, Time, Lifestyle, What Doesn't Show Up in Numbers, and Summary — to move from concrete to human
Used tables for scannability and callout quotes to flag the most important nuances
Kept tone honest and grounded without being pessimistic — explicitly acknowledged both the costs and the intangibles that resist quantification
Ended with six concrete conversation-starter questions designed to make the document immediately actionable for a couple
builder_result.companion_summary: The document is ready — a clean, honest, five-section breakdown of the real costs of having kids, covering financial figures (birth through college), time and career trade-offs, lifestyle changes, and the things that don't show up in any spreadsheet, plus six conversation-starter questions designed to make it immediately useful for the two of them.
builder_result.companion_tone_hint: Warm and grounded — this is a meaningful conversation they're preparing for, so honor the weight of it without being heavy-handed.
builder_result.user_next_action: Read through it together, use the six questions in Section 05 as a structured conversation guide, and customize the numbers for your specific city and situation — especially childcare costs, which vary dramatically by location.
